adventure.explanation: The event traverses iconic Alpine passes including Col de la Croix de Fer (2,067 meters) and Col du Glandon in the Maurienne valley, offering a multi-pass mountain experience across 118 km[2].

altitude.explanation: Riders summit to 2,067 meters at Col de la Croix de Fer's peak, then descend to 795 meters in the valley before climbing back to Vaujany at 1,245 meters, creating dramatic elevation changes[2].

climate.explanation: August racing in the French Alps typically offers mild conditions, though the event's mountain location means weather can be unpredictable; no specific climate data is provided for Vaujany's August conditions[1].

community.explanation: GFNY Alpes-Vaujany attracts international cyclists to the Vaujany resort, with the event being part of the globally-exported GFNY cyclosportive series that has expanded from its New York origins[1].

elevation.explanation: The long course features close to 4,000 meters of climbing over 118 km, with the Col de la Croix de Fer climb alone spanning 25 km and testing riders with steady 8% grades and maximum inclines of 14%[2].

expenses.explanation: While registration costs are not detailed in available sources, Vaujany offers accommodation at all budget levels and the event includes a mandatory official race jersey designed in NYC and made in Italy[4].

experience.explanation: Riders receive chip timing with results posted at gfny.cc/results, a mandatory official GFNY race jersey, and finish-line celebration in Vaujany after completing the challenging 118 km course[2][4].

field_depth.explanation: The long course is a competition with category rankings and podium awards, though specific field size data is unavailable; the medium course (60.8 km) is non-competitive with alphabetical result listings[2][4].

length.explanation: The long course spans 118 km with 4,000 meters of climbing, while a medium option covers 60.8 km, providing flexibility for riders of different abilities[2][4].

logistics.explanation: The event starts with a controlled 5 km descent from Vaujany (1,245m), includes aid stations along the course, and finishes in Vaujany village with post-race celebrations[2].

race_quality.explanation: The event features chip timing for accurate results, organized course marshaling with a controlled descent start, and multiple aid stations; the long course qualifies riders for category rankings and podium awards[2][4].

technicality.explanation: The final 5 km climb to Vaujany includes switchbacks with pitches up to 14%, and Col de la Croix de Fer's first 8 km average 8% with maximum inclines of 14%, requiring technical descending skills[2].

value.explanation: Riders receive chip timing, an official GFNY race jersey, and access to a 118 km course with 4,000 meters of elevation across three major Alpine passes, though specific entry fees are not disclosed[2][4].

cultural_impact.explanation: Part of Oisans cycling culture, echoing Tour de France legacy in the Alps.

prestige.explanation: GFNY Alpes-Vaujany is part of the international GFNY series but lacks the historical prominence of events like La Marmotte, which has run for nearly 30 years and attracts nearly 7,000 riders[3].

course_description.character: Brutal Alpine road sportive featuring iconic paved cols with relentless switchbacks, deep valleys, and high-altitude finishes in the French Oisans massif.

suffering_zones.label: Col de la Morte
suffering_zones.description: 15.1km at 6.7% average with 10% max; long, grinding ascent after initial descent.

suffering_zones.label: Col d’Ornon
suffering_zones.description: Tame start at 3% ramps to 7%; positioned before the marquee Alpe d'Huez battle.

suffering_zones.label: L’Alpe d’Huez
suffering_zones.description: Legendary 12km with 8%+ average and 21 switchbacks; the heart of suffering.

suffering_zones.label: Vaujany Final Climb
suffering_zones.description: Deceptive 5km kicker to the finish with 14% pitches; punishes early aggression.

course_description.signature_challenge: The Alpe d'Huez ascent followed by the Vaujany wall, testing pacing and recovery on Tour de France-proven roads.

logistics.overview: GFNY-organized event with long competitive course (151km/4172m) and medium non-competitive (60km); starts/finishes in Vaujany with aid stations and perks.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant chalets and hotels in Vaujany and nearby Alpe d'Huez; GFNY offers camping and VIP options.
logistics.getting_there: Fly into Grenoble (1.5hr drive) or Lyon (2.5hr); buses from Bourg d'Oisans.
logistics.parking: Designated areas in Vaujany village; car-free roads on event day for climbs.
logistics.spectator_info: Prime viewing at Alpe d'Huez switchbacks and Vaujany finish; roads reserved for cyclists.

climate.overview: Typical mid-June Alps: mild days, cool summits; prepare for rain and wind.
climate.altitude_effects: Hypoxia above 6000ft impacts power; hydrate aggressively.

terrain.terrain_overview: Fully paved roads through Oisans valleys and high cols; smooth asphalt on steep gradients.

history.overview: GFNY chapter in Vaujany leveraging Alpine icons like Alpe d'Huez; local benchmark evolving into international draw.
notable_editions: 2025 edition with long course over Col de la Morte and Ornon
Part of GFNY World Series qualifiers
history.growth_trajectory: Rapid rise via GFNY branding; from local sportive to global qualifier event.

final_verdict.one_liner: Alpine road suffering at its finest: Alpe d'Huez glory awaits the paced climber.
final_verdict.best_for: Endurance roadies seeking Tour cols and qualifiers; Marmotte prep riders.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: Gravel purists or flatland sprinters; budget travelers.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Save legs for Vaujany finish—Alpe d'Huez is just the appetizer.

citations.snippet: La Vaujany is our local trophy... 173km and 3,850m... benchmark race in the Alps.

citations.title: Course - GFNY La Vaujany
citations.snippet: Long Course... Col de la Morte... L’Alpe d’Huez... climb back up to Vaujany.
